attacking_style: Diesel-powered breakaway specialist who attacks early (30-40km out) on shallow climbs rather than waiting for steep finales. Wins from reduced sprints (3-12 riders) using positioning and experience over pure acceleration.
team_tactics: Minimal team support — operates independently in breaks and bridges. Has served super-domestique role for GC leaders (UAE 2023, communicating crosswinds). Team shelters him when targeting non-sprint objectives.
terrain_preference: Uphill sprint finales and shallow categorized climbs where diesel power sustains. Excels at tactical positioning in technical finishes (inside lines, corner entry). Struggles on explosive steep walls like Mur de Huy where absent from finale.
vulnerability: Lacks explosive acceleration for steep finale climbs (Mur de Huy, couldn't follow when breakaways attack aggressively). Misjudges effort distribution by going too early, then fading (Vuelta 2020 KOM loss). Time trials expose GC ambitions.
key_patterns: Serial breakaway rider: 3+ consecutive days in Tour 2019 hunting KOM through volume strategy
Bridging specialist: solo bridges from peloton to leaders on Ardennes climbs (LBL 2017, 2021)
Positioning over power: wins via corner lines and slipstream exploitation rather than watts (5+ instances)
Early/long-range attacks 30-40km out on shallow climbs, overextends on steep finales (Vuelta 2020 pattern)
Experience advantage in reduced sprints: 29-win composure beats younger riders in tactical finish
KOM classification hunting through opportunistic point collection, not pure climbing ability
form_trajectory: neutral: Limited 2023 data shows domestique role at Paris-Nice. Peak 2019-2020 with Tour KOM chase and Vuelta stage wins. No recent winning observations.
